articles.author: Lorian Belanger
articles.description: Across Iran’s capital, explosions rang out overnight as the U.S. and Israel pounded Iran with airstrikes. Iran and its allies have hit back against Israel and neighboring Gulf states.
articles.title: Live updates: Explosions heard in Tehran and Jerusalem on fifth day of war

articles.author: Bridget Brown, Lorian Belanger, Vanessa Gera
articles.description: Across Iran’s capital, explosions rang out overnight as the U.S. and Israel pounded Iran with airstrikes. Iran and its allies have hit back against Israel and neighboring Gulf states.
articles.title: Live updates: Iran moves to pick new supreme leader, Israel says he will be ‘a target for elimination'

articles.author: Phil Holm, WILL JARRETT, Kevin Vineys
articles.description: Airstrikes destroy entire blocks and compounds. Missiles soar across borders. Drones rain lethal destruction.
articles.title: The US-Israeli war with Iran in maps, graphics and images

articles.author: Jon Gambrell
articles.description: Mojtaba Khamenei, a son of Iran’s late Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, long has been considered a contender to the post of the country’s next paramount ruler — even before his father's death though he’s never being elected nor appointed to a government position. Khamenei, long a secretive figure within the Islamic Republic, hasn’t been seen publicly since Saturday. That's when an Israeli airstrike targeting the supreme leader’s offices killed his 86-year-old father and sparked the current war raging across the Middle East. That attack also killed his wife, Zahra Haddad Adel, who came from a family long associated with the country’s theocracy.
articles.title: Son of Iran's late supreme leader is a possible candidate to replace him

articles.author: Jon Gambrell, David Rising, Samy Magdy
articles.description: Explosions sounded in Iran’s capital as the war with the U.S. and Israel entered its fifth day with Israel targeting the Iranian leadership and security forces, and the Islamic Republic responding with missile barrages and swarms of drone attacks on Israel and across the Persian Gulf region. The Tehran blasts came at dawn Wednesday. Israel’s military said its air defenses had been activated to intercept Iranian missiles targeting Israel, with explosions heard around Jerusalem. With Iran's stranglehold on tanker movement through the Strait of Hormuz, Brent crude prices rose to more than $82 a barrel, up more than 13% since the start of the conflict and at its highest price since July 2024.
articles.title: Explosions sound in the Iranian capital and Jerusalem

articles.author: Steven Sloan
articles.description: Primary elections set up major Senate fights in Texas and North Carolina, and they also expose fresh tension over voting rules. North Carolina locked in a high-stakes matchup as Democrat Roy Cooper faces Republican Michael Whatley. The race helps decide Senate control in November. In Texas, Sen. John Cornyn heads to a May 26 runoff against Ken Paxton. Big money keeps flooding the contest. Voting also turned chaotic in Dallas and Williamson counties. New precinct rules confused voters and triggered lawsuits.
articles.title: Election 2026: Takeaways from first votes of the campaign

articles.author: Thomas Beaumont, Will Weissert
articles.description: State Rep. James Talarico topped Congresswoman Jasmine Crockett in Texas' closely watched Democratic Senate primary. He'll face the winner of a May runoff for the Republican nomination between Texas Sen. John Cornyn and Attorney General Ken Paxton. Cornyn and Paxton were the top finishers in a three-way contest Tuesday with Rep. Wesley Hunt. No GOP candidate hit the 50% vote threshold needed to win outright. Texas, along with North Carolina and Arkansas, on Tuesday kicked off midterm elections with control of Congress at stake and against the backdrop of the U.S.-Israeli war with Iran.
articles.title: Talarico wins Texas Senate Democratic nomination while Cornyn and Paxton head to Republican runoff

articles.author: Sean Murphy
articles.description: Republican Rep. Tony Gonzales of Texas was forced into a primary runoff with his GOP opponent as he faces calls to resign over allegations of having an affair with an aide. The three-term congressman is now in a rematch against Brandon Herrera, a gun manufacturer who narrowly lost to Gonzales in the 2024 primaries. Neither candidate secured more than 50% of the vote in the four-way primary to win outright. Gonzales entered the nation’s first big primary of 2026 under pressure from fellow House Republicans after published reports last month that alleged to show explicit text messages between him and the former staffer. Gonzales has said he won’t step down.
articles.title: GOP Rep. Tony Gonzales dragged into primary runoff after affair allegations
